commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: To his own place of perdition, which he brought himself to. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Oecumenius
commentaries.content: When he betrayed his Lord and sought no forgiveness for this his great sin, Judas chose to himself perdition. Here, then, his own place or lot means suicide. So when we read Balaam that 'he rose up and went and returned to his place,' (Num 24:25) this is said not only of a local removal, but of a moral one.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
